series.series_description:
Standard tournament arc structure. 52 planned chapters, 4 arcs.
Chapter_end_hooks must be new_rival or revelation family (no soft close on weekly serialization).
The aspiration engine requires the protagonist to VISIBLY improve — power levels
must feel earned. Avoid power-up-via-emotion without shown training basis (Naruto
late-arc failure mode).
Observer dialogue ("Impossible...!") is load-bearing genre content, not noise.
series.reader_promise: You will feel the specific electricity of someone becoming who they were always meant to be.

series.series_description:
This profile demonstrates the core distinction between shojo and josei grammar:
1. No decorative visual effects (sparkle, flower, chibi deformation)
2. Emotional precision over emotional performance
3. Restraint-over-exposition: the reader fills the silence with their own longing
4. Realistic workplace detail matters — the reader LIVES this
5. Chapter hooks are almost_confession family, not new_rival or revelation
   (no manufactured drama; the emotional territory IS the drama)

The MDLG-11 (restraint_over_exposition) gate should treat this series as
the canonical test case for josei grammar compliance.

Do not let the Chapter Writer add exclamation marks to internal monologue.
Josei characters think in period-ending sentences.
series.reader_promise: You will feel the exact particular ache of wanting someone you cannot have at work.

series.series_description:
This series is for readers who have been told their anxiety is "not that bad."
The visual grammar does the work: long silent sequences + sparse dialogue +
restrained line work = the reader feels their own nervous system slow down.
Do not add dramatic chapter hooks. The mood_based engine is intentional.
The emotional engine (cozy_restoration) means readers finish each chapter
feeling safer, not more activated.
series.reader_promise: You will feel like someone finally understands what exhaustion really is.
